title: INVESTIGATION: why did check_lock not block substrate-worker-2's Edit writes without a live session registration? (P-002 liveness question)
description: Operator-ordered investigation (2026-07-05). Facts: substrate-worker-2 was unregistered after T-033 (~02:15Z); T-037 ran ~02:50-03:00Z holding locks as substrate-worker-2 on .claude/agents/orchestration-planner.md and .harness/plan.md; its content landed and the verifier confirmed lock discipline - yet check_lock.py should refuse an Edit when ambient identity (main) != holder (substrate-worker-2) and holder is not a live session holder. Determine WHICH hypothesis is true with evidence: (H1) the PreToolUse hook does not fire for subagent tool calls; (H2) the hook fired and refused, and the worker fell back to Bash-based writes (bash-dodge - grep transcript.jsonl for the T-037 window: Edit attempts vs Bash heredoc/python writes on those paths); (H3) check_lock fail-open exception path swallowed an error; (H4) session registration WAS live (check session_holders.json history via events.jsonl register/unregister events and TTLs - was there an unexpired earlier registration?); (H5) the lock had already been released/expired by edit time (compare lock acquire/release timestamps vs the Edit timestamps in transcript.jsonl); (H6) other (state it). Method: transcript.jsonl is the ground truth for tool calls (grep tool_name Edit/Write with those file paths + timestamps); events.jsonl for lock/session lifecycle; re-run check_lock.py with synthetic payloads reproducing the exact state to confirm the identified path. Deliverable: a REPRODUCIBLE root-cause note appended to this task (multi-line notes ok) + a falsifiable fix proposal IF a real gap exists (or 'works as designed' verdict with the reasoning). PROPOSE only, do not fix. Handoff to verifier.

notes.note: Investigation start: reading check_lock.py + harness_common.session_holders(); will extract T-037 window from events.jsonl, grep transcript.jsonl for Edit/Write on orchestration-planner.md + plan.md, then reproduce state with synthetic PreToolUse payloads.

ROOT-CAUSE (reproducible). VERDICT: H4 CONFIRMED - the session registration WAS live at edit time; the task premise 'WITHOUT a live session registration' is FACTUALLY FALSE. WORKS AS DESIGNED; no protection gap in T-037. One latent authorization gap flagged separately.

SMOKING-GUN TIMELINE (all 2026-07-05, session 15636cc2=MAIN, agent=main):
- events.jsonl:677-678  03:08:07Z lock_acquired orchestration-planner.md + plan.md, holder=substrate-worker-2, ttl=900 (=> expire 03:23:07).
- transcript:1157 / events.jsonl:693  03:08:38Z  Bash 'session.py register substrate-worker-2 --by substrate-worker-2' -> session_holder_registered substrate-worker-2, expires 2026-07-05T05:08:38Z.
- transcript:1161,1163  03:08:52Z,03:09:00Z  Edit orchestration-planner.md (PostToolUse, structuredPatch resp).
- transcript:1168,1171  03:09:15Z,03:09:27Z  Edit plan.md (PostToolUse, structuredPatch resp).
- events.jsonl  03:10:11Z lock_released both files.
All 4 Edits fell 14-49s AFTER the 03:08:38 registration AND inside the live lock window. So check_lock evaluated: me=main (ambient), holder=substrate-worker-2, lock live+unexpired, holder IN session_holders() -> return 0 (ALLOW). Exactly the P-002 design.

WHY THE OPERATOR WAS MISLED: they saw events.jsonl:622 (02:16:15Z unregister of substrate-worker-2) and the stated ~02:50-03:00 window, but MISSED events.jsonl:693 (03:08:38Z self-re-registration) which is logged agent=main and buried among probe-agent-x churn (lines 688-702). Actual T-037 claim was 03:08:05, not 02:50.

REPRODUCTION (isolated scratch repo, real bin/ copied, ROOT reparented; synthetic PreToolUse payloads piped to check_lock.py):
- Scenario A  lock held by substrate-worker-2, NO registration, me=main  -> EXIT 2 BLOCK  (proves the gate is real; unregistered would self-block).
- Scenario B  same lock, LIVE registration of substrate-worker-2, me=main -> EXIT 0 ALLOW (ACTUAL edit-time state; matches observed).
- Scenario C  same lock, NO registration, me=substrate-worker-2 (holder==me) -> EXIT 0 ALLOW.
- Scenario D  same lock, EXPIRED registration, me=main -> EXIT 2 BLOCK (registration expiry enforced).

HYPOTHESES RULED OUT:
- H1 (hook doesnt fire for subagents): NOT operative. Edits ran in MAIN session (session_id 15636cc2, agent=main) via per-call identity override, not a separate Task subagent. Hook fires for main-session calls: PostToolUse transcript lines are hook-produced, and audit_gen1 G2:48 documents check_lock SELF-BLOCKING (exit 2) exactly this holder=substrate-worker-2 vs me=main case before P-002.
- H2 (bash-dodge): FALSE. transcript Bash calls in window (1149,1150,1169,1174,1175,1178,1183,1186) are only lock.py/blackboard.py/git-diff/grep; the 4 content changes are genuine Edit-tool calls with structuredPatch responses. No heredoc/python writes to those paths.
- H3 (fail-open exception): NOT implicated. Scenario B returns 0 via the clean 'holder in session_holders()' branch, not the except handler.
- H5 (lock expired/released by edit time): FALSE. acquire 03:08:07 + ttl 900 = expire 03:23:07; released 03:10:11; all edits 03:08:52-03:09:27 within live window.

DECISION: T-037 behaved correctly - a coordinated in-session worker (substrate-worker-2) was whitelisted in session_holders() before editing its own locked files, so the P-002 fix suppressed the self-block exactly as intended. No fix required for the reported symptom.

DECISION (latent gap, separate from T-037 symptom): session.py register() [session.py:58-81] enforces NO authorization on --by/registrar. transcript:1157 shows the worker self-registered ('--by substrate-worker-2'). harness_common.session_holders() [harness_common.py:150-178] then honors ANY live entry as a lock-bypass. Docstrings promise 'cross-session/cross-engine holders are NEVER auto-registered ... their locks still block mechanically', but session.py register lets ANY caller opt ITSELF (or any name) in -> the coordinator-only-registration invariant is an unenforced social convention.

FALSIFIABLE FIX PROPOSAL (PROPOSE ONLY; register with coordinator, do not apply): P-042A 'Mechanical authorization for session-holder registration'. Target: .harness/bin/session.py register() + harness_common.session_holders(). Mutation: gate register() to a coordinator allowlist (e.g. require ambient CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID in a small coordinator set, or --by==ambient id AND id allowlisted), log session_holder_register_refused on violation, and stamp registrar id on the entry so session_holders() can honor only coordinator-registered names. Measurable/falsifiable: replay 'rogue' seq 'CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=gemini-runner session.py register gemini-runner' -> must exit non-zero and NOT appear in session_holders(); then synthetic PreToolUse Edit (me=main) on a gemini-runner-held lock must return EXIT 2. TODAY that exact sequence yields EXIT 0 (bypass). Success metric: non-coordinator self-registrations allowed drops 1->0; cross-session lock bypass via self-registration becomes unreachable; T-037-style legitimate in-session whitelisting still returns EXIT 0 when done by the coordinator.

OPEN-QUESTION (for verifier/coordinator): Is worker self-registration ('--by substrate-worker-2') an accepted pattern for in-session coordinated workers, or must fable-5-coordinator/main always be the sole registrar? P-042A assumes the latter; if the former is intended, P-042A must instead scope the allowlist to same-session registrars (add session_id to entries) rather than a coordinator-name allowlist.
OPEN-QUESTION (observability, minor): session_holder_registered events do not carry a task_id/correlation and log agent=main, which is what let the operator misread the timeline. Optional: add task_id to session.py register + log_event.

HANDOFF -> verifier: VERDICT H4 (works-as-designed): substrate-worker-2 self-registered at 03:08:38Z (transcript:1157, events.jsonl:693, expires 05:08:38) BEFORE all 4 Edits (transcript:1161/1163 orchestration-planner.md 03:08:52/03:09:00; 1168/1171 plan.md 03:09:15/03:09:27), inside the live lock (events.jsonl:677-678 acquire 03:08:07 ttl900; released 03:10:11). check_lock returned 0 via 'holder in session_holders()'. Operator premise 'no live registration' is false (missed events.jsonl:693). Reproduced 4 scenarios in isolated scratch repo piping synthetic PreToolUse payloads to check_lock.py: A(no reg,me=main)=EXIT2 block; B(live reg,me=main)=EXIT0 allow=actual; C(holder==me)=EXIT0; D(expired reg)=EXIT2. H1/H2/H3/H5 ruled out with cited lines. NO gap in T-037. Latent gap flagged: session.py register() has no auth on --by (worker self-whitelisted) -> falsifiable proposal P-042A registered in the task note for coordinator to enter evolution.pending_proposals. To reproduce: copy .harness/bin/{check_lock,harness_common}.py into a scratch repo, write a live lock (holder=substrate-worker-2) + toggle session_holders.json, pipe {"tool_name":"Edit","tool_input":{"file_path":"<scratch>/.claude/agents/orchestration-planner.md"}} with CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=main.

VERIFIED (verifier-b, independent replay). H4 root-cause CONFIRMED; investigation ACCEPTED. (1) TIMELINE replayed against ground-truth logs — every cited line orders exactly as claimed: events:622 unregister 02:16:15Z < events:677-678 lock_acquired 03:08:07Z (holder=substrate-worker-2,T-037,ttl900=>expire 03:23:07) < events:693/transcript:1157 'session.py register substrate-worker-2 --by substrate-worker-2' 03:08:38Z (expires 05:08:38) < transcript:1161/1163 Edit planner.md 03:08:52/03:09:00 < 1168/1171 Edit plan.md 03:09:15/03:09:27 (all 4 have PostToolUse structuredPatch responses) < events:713-714 lock_released 03:10:11Z. All 4 edits fell inside the live lock AND after the self-re-registration. (2) FOUR-SCENARIO repro rebuilt from scratch (my own driver, real bin/ copied, mktemp ROOT): A(no reg,me=main)=EXIT2, B(live reg,me=main)=EXIT0, C(holder==me)=EXIT0, D(expired reg,me=main)=EXIT2 — matches producer exactly. (3) H2 bash-dodge FALSE: pulled full commands for every Bash call in window 1145-1190 — all are lock.py/blackboard.py/git-diff/grep/read-only python (L1174 opens plan.md with .read() for an assert, not a write); zero heredoc/open('w')/tee to planner.md or plan.md; only writes to those paths are the 4 genuine Edit calls. H1/G2 citation confirmed: audit_gen1.md line 48 IS the G2 row documenting the pre-P-002 self-block (exit 2, holder=substrate-worker-2 vs me=main). (4) LATENT GAP confirmed code-accurate: session.py register() [58-81] + harness_common.session_holders() [150-178] enforce NO authorization. Live-bin repro: 'CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=fake-cross-engine session.py register fake-cross-engine' -> EXIT0, appears in list with by=fake-cross-engine (self-whitelist). Scratch bypass: same fake-cross-engine-held lock -> CONTROL(unregistered,me=main)=EXIT2 then after self-register=EXIT0 (bypass reproduced). CLEANED UP: unregistered fake-cross-engine; live registry restored to original 3 (evolution-analyst/orchestration-planner/fable-5-coordinator, all by=main). (5) P-042A is FALSIFIABLE (concrete before/after exit codes + success metric + T-037-regression guard); both OPEN-QUESTIONs valid and correctly tagged. THREAT-MODEL NOTE for coordinator when formalizing P-042A: success-metric wording 'cross-session lock bypass via self-registration becomes unreachable' overclaims for a local-files/fail-open trust model — anyone can hand-edit session_holders.json directly. The DECISION-note framing ('unenforced social convention') is honest; recommend tightening the metric to scope the guarantee to the sanctioned register() CLI / accidental protocol-following self-whitelisting, explicitly out-of-scoping direct file edits (same trust boundary as the fail-open hook). Also flag: P-042A's two rule sub-variants diverge on the actual T-037 evidence (ambient was main, --by was substrate-worker-2) — 'ambient in coordinator set' PRESERVES T-037, '--by==ambient AND allowlisted' BREAKS it; OPEN-QUESTION 1 (session_id-scoped allowlist) is the reconciling path. (6) DISCIPLINE: git diff .harness/bin/ empty (PROPOSE-only honored); notes carry DECISION:/OPEN-QUESTION: taxonomy; handoff not self-done (events:858 handoff to verifier by evolution-analyst). HOUSEKEEPING: lock.py status shows orchestration-planner holds LIVE locks on plan.md + T-044..T-052 under T-043 (acquired 04:16-04:19) — legitimate active planner, NOT swept per instruction; no expired leases.
